Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, and ketonuria, suggesting possible renal impairment and metabolic stress. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, and contains glucose, protein, bilirubin, and ketones, but no nitrites or leukocytes, making infection less likely. HbA1c is low, indicating no chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further evaluation of renal function and possible causes of proteinuria and ketonuria.
